css: #dynamic-content .prompt-card { background: linear-gradient(135deg, #fdfbfb 0%, #f6eff0 100%); width: 100%; max-width: 100%; border-radius: 0px; box-shadow: 0 15px 45px rgba(0,0,0,0.05), 0 1px 3px rgba(0,0,0,0.02); padding: 2.5rem 10px; margin: 0 auto 3rem; } #dynamic-content .pin-title { font-size: inherit; line-height: 1.3; letter-spacing: normal; margin-top: 0; margin-bottom: 2rem; width: 100%; max-width: 100%; text-align: center; } #dynamic-content .description-container { margin-bottom: 2.5rem; text-align: center; border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.05); padding-bottom: 1.5rem; } #dynamic-content .description-text { font-size: 1.05rem; color: #444; line-height: 1.6; font-style: italic; max-width: 44rem; margin: 0 auto; text-align: left; padding: 0 1.25rem; } #dynamic-content .prompt-list { width: 100%; margin: 0 auto; } #dynamic-content .prompt-item { display: flex; align-items: flex-start; width: 100%; margin-bottom: 2rem; gap: 0.5rem; } #dynamic-content .prompt-number { font-size: 1.1rem; font-weight: 800; color: #b8b8b8; padding-top: 2px; min-width: 1.5rem; text-align: left; } #dynamic-content .prompt-text { font-size: 1.05rem; font-weight: 600; color: #222; line-height: 1.6; flex: 1; } @media (min-width: 768px) { #dynamic-content .prompt-card { max-width: 52rem; border-radius: 1.5rem; padding: 4.5rem 3.5rem; margin: 3rem auto 5rem; } #dynamic-content .pin-title { margin-bottom: 2.5rem; } #dynamic-content .description-container { margin-bottom: 3.5rem; padding-bottom: 2rem; } #dynamic-content .prompt-item { gap: 1.25rem; margin-bottom: 2.75rem; } #dynamic-content .prompt-number { font-size: 1.25rem; min-width: 2.2rem; text-align: right; } #dynamic-content .prompt-text { font-size: 1.15rem; } }
html:
12 Compassionate Journal Prompts for Navigating Grief

Grief is a deeply personal journey with no set timeline. These 12 prompts offer a gentle space to honor your feelings, remember what you have lost, and find small moments of peace as you move through the healing process.

1.
What is one specific memory of your loved one that always brings a smile to your face? Describe the sights and sounds of that moment and why it remains so special to you.
2.
What is one thing you wish you could say to your loved one right now? Write those words down and reflect on how expressing them helps you feel more at peace.
3.
What is a specific quality or lesson your loved one possessed that you admire? How can you honor them by practicing that same quality in your own life today?
4.
How does your grief feel in your body at this very moment? Describe the physical sensation and reflect on how acknowledging it helps you stay present with your feelings.
5.
In what way have you grown or changed since experiencing this loss? Reflect on a new strength you have discovered within yourself while navigating this difficult time.
6.
What is a small comfort, such as a song or a specific place, that helps you feel connected to your loved one? Describe how this connection brings you a moment of calm.
7.
Think of a recent moment when you felt a sense of connection to your loved one. What was happening, and how did that experience help you feel supported?
8.
What is one thing regarding this loss that you have been holding against yourself? Describe how it would feel to replace that guilt with a sense of compassion for your own journey.
9.
What is one small, meaningful way you can honor your loved one's memory this week? Reflect on how this act of remembrance brings a sense of purpose to your day.
10.
What does 'healing' look like to you today? Identify one small step you have taken recently that shows you are moving forward, even in a small way.
11.
Identify three things in your life that still feel steady or 'whole' right now. How does focusing on these constants help you find your footing when things feel uncertain?
12.
What is one thing you are looking forward to in the coming days, no matter how small? Describe how this focus on the future helps you find a sense of hope today.
